name: Cannoli Alla Siciliana
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Wine (or Marsala, Burgundy, or Vegetable Oil)
1/2 square unsweetened Chocolate (grated) or 1/2 tablespoon Cocoa (optional)
3 tablespoons Orange peel, candied and chopped
recipeInstructions:
To make cannoli shells, you'll need 3 or 4 metal tubes, preferably made from very light tin, about 7 inches long and 1 1/8 inches in diameter. The edges should not be soldered (old aluminum chairs cut into pieces work well).
1. Combine flour, shortening, sugar, and salt, gradually adding wine until a firm dough or paste forms.
2. Form the dough into a ball, cover with cloth, and let stand for about 1 hour.
3. Cut the dough in half and roll one half into a thin sheet about 1/4 inch thick.
4. Cut the sheet into 4-inch squares. Place a metal tube diagonally across each square from one point to another, wrapping the dough around the tube by overlapping the two points and sealing the overlapping points with a little egg white.
5. Meanwhile, heat vegetable oil in a large deep pan for frying. Drop one or two tubes at a time into hot oil. Fry gently until the shells are golden brown.
6. Remove from the pan, let cool, and gently remove the shell from the metal tube.
7. Repeat the procedure until all shells are made.
Filling: Mix ricotta thoroughly with sifted dry ingredients (cinnamon, cocoa, vanilla, and fruit peel). Add a little grated pistachio if desired. Chill in the refrigerator before filling shells. Fill cold cannoli shells; smooth the filling evenly at each end of the shell. Decorate each end with a piece of glace cherry and sprinkle shells with confectioners' sugar. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
These are best when filled just before your company arrives.
